design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: New flagship topic: critical-overlap tail exponent for sharp partial identification of treatment effects under positivity failure. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchors are Khan-Tamer 2010 on irregular identification/support and inverse weighting, Rothe-style robust inference under limited overlap, Hong-Leung-Li finite-population limited-overlap inference, and recent non-overlap ATE bounds. Our theorem is not that because it is not an estimator or trimming rule and not the generic statement that non-overlap widens Manski bounds: the focal object is an overlap-tail profile alpha(P) and an extremal-law certificate showing an exact phase threshold for the sharp identified-set width of CATT/ATE under bounded outcomes and observed covariate-propensity tail mass. Require a concrete nonroutine object: a finite/two-tail witness family with the same nominal overlap support but different polynomial tail exponents, an explicit endpoint-attaining sequence, and an iff threshold separating finite-width, polynomial blow-up, and vacuous regimes. If the proposal reduces to ordinary support coverage, overlap weights, Manski missing-cell bounds, trimming, or generic LP duality without the exponent witness and endpoint certificate, pivot or stop early.

proposed_from.topic: New flagship topic: critical-overlap tail exponent for sharp partial identification of treatment effects under positivity failure. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchors are Khan-Tamer 2010 on irregular identification/support and inverse weighting, Rothe-style robust inference under limited overlap, Hong-Leung-Li finite-population limited-overlap inference, and recent non-overlap ATE bounds. Our theorem is not that because it is not an estimator or trimming rule and not the generic statement that non-overlap widens Manski bounds: the focal object is an overlap-tail profile alpha(P) and an extremal-law certificate showing an exact phase threshold for the sharp identified-set width of CATT/ATE under bounded outcomes and observed covariate-propensity tail mass. Require a concrete nonroutine object: a finite/two-tail witness family with the same nominal overlap support but different polynomial tail exponents, an explicit endpoint-attaining sequence, and an iff threshold separating finite-width, polynomial blow-up, and vacuous regimes. If the proposal reduces to ordinary support coverage, overlap weights, Manski missing-cell bounds, trimming, or generic LP duality without the exponent witness and endpoint certificate, pivot or stop early.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art is CausalSmith doc/API.md Section 3.1 `ManskiNonparametricBounds_Study`, which records bounded-outcome Manski-style treatment-effect bounds and level-set restrictions. The proposed v1 differs by naming the observable overlap-tail profile alpha(P), constructing a two-tail exponent witness, and separating ATT one-tail from ATE two-tail normalized width. The failed bank entry `pid_critical_overlap_exponent/v1` is not prior theorem art because it failed before proposal review; `pid_heavy_tail_cate_bounds/v1` is used only to avoid the prior concrete-witness and moment-boundary defects.","published_axis":"Closest published papers split into estimator/inference tail work and partial-ID mass/pooling work. KhanTamer2010IrregularIdentification and HeilerKazak2021ExtremePropensity study inverse-weight rates and limiting laws, while Rothe2017LimitedOverlapCI and HongLeungLi2020FinitePopulationLimitedOverlap study inference under limited overlap. Manski1990NonparametricBounds, LeeWeidner2026LimitedPoolingBounds, and SusmannMcCleanDiaz2025NonOverlapATEBounds provide bounded-outcome, pooling, or non-overlap-mass bounds. The epsilon-gap is the explicit observable alpha(P) phase law with endpoint-attaining two-tail laws that keep nominal overlap support fixed."}
seed_list: Characterize the overlap-tail profile alpha(P) and prove an iff phase law for sharp CATT profile-width amplification under bounded outcomes.
Construct endpoint-attaining two-tail laws with identical nominal overlap support but different polynomial tail exponents and different sharp CATT widths.
Separate one-sided CATT/ATT tail width from two-sided ATE tail width by proving which tail exponents enter each sharp profile interval.
Repair the heavy-tail CATE converse by replacing finite truncation with an endpoint-approaching untruncated witness sequence.
State a finite-first-moment boundary theorem separating undefined means, finite width, and vacuity for tail-restricted CATE bounds.
Give a non-LP support-function representation of non-overlap ATE bounds in terms of the overlap-tail profile.
Show when limited-pooling bounds collapse to Manski bounds or strict tail-profile bounds as the pooling radius vanishes.
Define a critical tail-reporting diagnostic for observational studies: same non-overlap mass, different exponent, different bound width.
Prove an open-class sharpness result for overlap-tail vacuity rather than a single hand-tuned weak-overlap law.
Compare estimator-rate tail exponents with profile-width exponents and prove they are generally non-equivalent objects.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize the overlap-tail profile alpha(P) and prove an iff phase law for sharp CATT profile-width amplification under bounded outcomes.

anchor_paper.author: Khan and Tamer
anchor_paper.claim: Inverse-weight identification can be irregular, with rates depending on relative tail conditions.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: This rate depends on relative tail conditions

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens support/overlap, but non-trivial because the object is a sharp profile-width exponent, not a coverage rule.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from KhanTamer2010IrregularIdentification because inverse-weight tails already determine the difficulty.
seed_details.rebuttal: Khan-Tamer determine estimator-rate irregularity; the seed determines sharp bounded-outcome profile-width phases with endpoint certificates.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not KhanTamer2010IrregularIdentification because alpha(P) governs sharp identified-set width, not convergence of an IPW estimator.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: the focal object is a new profile-width exponent plus endpoint extremal-law certificate, not generic support coverage.

adversarial_referee.instance: The width object could look like ordinary Manski missing-cell width.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The draft names alpha(P), amplification width A(t;P), and two worked exponent witnesses.

adversarial_referee.instance: The iff threshold may only prove sufficiency.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The proposal labels the iff as Conjecture and makes necessity the endpoint-law construction.

adversarial_referee.instance: A single tail law might be hand-tuned.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Section 9 gives a q-family with same nominal support and varying exponents.

seed_details.one_line: Construct endpoint-attaining two-tail laws with identical nominal overlap support but different polynomial tail exponents and different sharp CATT widths.

anchor_paper.author: Lee and Weidner
anchor_paper.claim: Limited pooling bounds handle violated overlap but do not give an exponent-varying extremal-law certificate.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: when the overlap condition is violated

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/witness seed is strengthened by an open q-family rather than one degenerate law.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a Manski endpoint construction because bounded outcomes always attain extremes.
seed_details.rebuttal: The novelty is not endpoint existence alone; it is same-support exponent variation changing profile amplification.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not LeeWeidner2026LimitedPoolingBounds because no limited-pooling architecture is imposed.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: the certificate varies the exponent while holding nominal support fixed.

adversarial_referee.instance: The q-family might only be a normalization artifact.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Exhibit 9.2 computes mu, nu, and alpha from the same U support.

adversarial_referee.instance: Lee-Weidner may already encode the same tail geometry through pooling.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Their object is pooling bounds; the seed's profile exponent is not a pooling radius.

adversarial_referee.instance: No computable focal object if alpha is only asserted.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: alpha(P) is computed directly from mu(t) and nu(t) in Section 9.

seed_details.one_line: Separate one-sided CATT/ATT tail width from two-sided ATE tail width by proving which tail exponents enter each sharp profile interval.

anchor_paper.author: Hong, Leung, and Li
anchor_paper.claim: Limited overlap is modeled by propensity scores degenerating to zero or one.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: sending the propensity score to zero (or one) with the sample size

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target variation is strengthened because CATT and ATE use different tail profiles, not just different labels.
seed_details.referee_objection: ATT versus ATE tail asymmetry is a routine decomposition of missing cells.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed asks for the exact profile frontier and open-class asymmetric-tail witnesses.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not HongLeungLi2020FinitePopulationLimitedOverlap because the target is sharp interval width, not estimator convergence.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: adds asymmetric-tail frontier and open-class witness.

adversarial_referee.instance: If CATT is covered, ATT, ATU, and ATE may also be covered.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The draft states CATT and ATE and flags the one-tail/two-tail family distinction.

adversarial_referee.instance: The frontier may prove only one direction.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Necessity is assigned to asymmetric two-tail construction in Section 10.

adversarial_referee.instance: Hong-Leung-Li study finite-population rates, not PI bounds.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The draft says exactly that and uses them only as a limited-overlap rate comparator.

seed_details.one_line: Repair the heavy-tail CATE converse by replacing finite truncation with an endpoint-approaching untruncated witness sequence.

anchor_paper.author: prior CausalSmith reviewer
anchor_paper.claim: Prior reviewer required an actual untruncated non-integrable law for the converse witness.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Repair the converse witness by exhibiting an actual untruncated non-integrable law

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample seed is stronger only if promoted from finite truncations to endpoint sequence.
seed_details.referee_objection: This repeats the failed heavy-tail proposal.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed directly targets the reviewer-specified missing witness rather than the rejected alpha=1 claim.

seed_details.one_line: State a finite-first-moment boundary theorem separating undefined means, finite width, and vacuity for tail-restricted CATE bounds.

anchor_paper.author: prior CausalSmith reviewer
anchor_paper.claim: Prior reviewer rejected alpha=1 as the vacuity boundary.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: the true boundary is not 'alpha=1 gives vacuity'

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary seed avoids alpha=1 tautology by separating mean existence from interval width.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a correction of an internal failed proposal, not a publishable paper.
seed_details.rebuttal: It would need a published Gamma-selection or tail-bound comparator before promotion.

seed_details.one_line: Give a non-LP support-function representation of non-overlap ATE bounds in terms of the overlap-tail profile.

anchor_paper.author: Susmann, McClean, and Diaz
anchor_paper.claim: Non-overlap ATE bounds have width proportional to non-overlap subpopulation size.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: width proportional to the size of the non-overlap subpopulation

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Default-to-LP avoided by using a support-function/profile representation.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is generic support-function duality for Manski bounds.
seed_details.rebuttal: The support function must be indexed by the exponent profile and endpoint witnesses.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not SusmannMcCleanDiaz2025NonOverlapATEBounds because the width varies with tail exponent at fixed nominal support.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier unless the support-function object is made nonroutine.

seed_details.one_line: Show when limited-pooling bounds collapse to Manski bounds or strict tail-profile bounds as the pooling radius vanishes.

anchor_paper.author: Lee and Weidner
anchor_paper.claim: Limited pooling interpolates between no pooling and inverse-propensity weighting.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: only using limited pooling of information across observations

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Bridge seed is weaker unless it proves a sharp non-equivalence frontier.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is an asymptotic restatement of Lee-Weidner's tuning parameter.
seed_details.rebuttal: Only survives if it yields a strict tail-profile collapse criterion.

seed_details.one_line: Define a critical tail-reporting diagnostic for observational studies: same non-overlap mass, different exponent, different bound width.

anchor_paper.author: Crump, Hotz, Imbens, and Mitnik
anchor_paper.claim: Trimming changes the target population to handle limited overlap.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: changing the focus to average treatment effects for a subset

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Applied diagnostic seed is weaker without a theorem-level phase object.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a reporting recommendation, not a theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: It can be a corollary of the chosen phase theorem, not the flagship kernel.

seed_details.one_line: Prove an open-class sharpness result for overlap-tail vacuity rather than a single hand-tuned weak-overlap law.

anchor_paper.author: prior CausalSmith reviewer
anchor_paper.claim: Prior reviewer rejected a weak-star-open vacuity class without a concrete law.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: The weak-star-open vacuity class has no concrete non-corner law

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Witness seed is stronger if it proves an open class, weaker if one-law only.
seed_details.referee_objection: This repeats an internal reviewer fix.
seed_details.rebuttal: The overlap-tail version would be tied to published limited-overlap anchors.

seed_details.one_line: Compare estimator-rate tail exponents with profile-width exponents and prove they are generally non-equivalent objects.

anchor_paper.author: Heiler and Kazak
anchor_paper.claim: Extreme propensity scores can produce alpha-stable limits for IPW/DR inference.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Bridge seed is strong only if it gives a formal non-equivalence, not a prose distinction.
seed_details.referee_objection: Estimator-rate exponents and width exponents are obviously different by definition.
seed_details.rebuttal: Needs a same-law comparator showing one finite while the other diverges.

proposed_from.literature_map: Stage -1.1 map reused: Khan and Tamer (2010, Econometrica) connect inverse-weight identification to relative tail conditions; Rothe (2017, Econometrica) studies limited-overlap confidence intervals through small local sample sizes; Hong, Leung, and Li (2020, Econometrics Journal) study degenerating finite-population propensity scores and slower rates; Heiler and Kazak (2021, JoE) study extreme propensity scores and stable limits; Crump et al. (2009, Biometrika) and Li et al. (2018, JASA) change the estimand toward overlap populations; Manski (1990, AER) gives bounded-outcome missing-cell bounds; Imbens and Manski (2004, Econometrica) study inference for interval-identified parameters; Lee and Weidner (2026, JoE) give limited-pooling ATE/ATT bounds; Susmann, McClean, and Diaz (2025, arXiv) give non-overlap ATE bounds with width tied to non-overlap mass. The open gap is a sharp partial-ID width phase law indexed by propensity-tail exponents with endpoint extremal witnesses.
proposed_from.last_draft_handoff: {"status":"completed","mode":"pivot","version":1,"message":"Two-tail overlap laws with identical nominal support conjecturally have sharp weak-overlap contribution width governed by the minimum propensity-tail exponent.","literature_map":"Inherited from Stage -1.1 gaps context. Khan and Tamer (2010, Econometrica, doi:10.3982/ECTA7372) connect irregular inverse-weight identification to relative tail conditions. Rothe (2017, Econometrica) studies robust confidence intervals under limited overlap. Hong, Leung, and Li (2020, Econometrics Journal) formalize finite-population overlap degeneration and effective-sample-size rates. Heiler and Kazak (2021, Journal of Econometrics) study IPW/DR limits with many extreme propensity scores. Crump, Hotz, Imbens, and Mitnik (2009, Biometrika) and Li, Morgan, and Zaslavsky (2018, JASA) change the estimand through trimming or overlap weighting. Manski (1990, AER) and Imbens-Manski (2004, Econometrica) are the bounded-outcome and interval-ID benchmarks. Lee and Weidner (2026, Journal of Econometrics, doi:10.1016/j.jeconom.2026.106254) give limited-pooling ATE/ATT bounds under overlap violation. Susmann, McClean, and Diaz (2025, arXiv:2509.20206) give non-overlap ATE bounds driven by non-overlap mass. The live gap is a sharp partial-ID phase law certified by exponent-varying two-tail witnesses, not estimator rates, trimming, pooling, or generic Manski mass bounds.","seeds":["Characterize the
